---
title: "Backends"
---

A backend consumes the augmented dataset (per-row quadratic and linear coefficients $a_k$, $b_k$ produced by `rieszreg.build_augmented`) and a `LossSpec`, and returns a fitted `Predictor`. Two implementation packages ship today; both satisfy `rieszreg.backends.base.Backend` and slot into `RieszEstimator` interchangeably.

## Pick a backend

| | [Boosting (`rieszboost`)](boosting.qmd) | [Kernel ridge (`krrr`)](kernel.qmd) |
|---|---|---|
| **Method** | Gradient boosting (xgboost or sklearn) | Closed-form solve of $(\mathrm{diag}(a) K + n\lambda I)\gamma = -b/2$ |
| **Algorithm** | Lee & Schuler ([2501.04871](https://arxiv.org/abs/2501.04871)) | Singh ([2102.11076](https://arxiv.org/abs/2102.11076)) |
| **Best for** | Tabular data, $n \gtrsim 1{,}000$, mixed feature types | Smooth $\alpha_0$, small/medium $n$, low-dimensional $X$ |
| **Iterative?** | Yes (rounds + early stopping) | No (closed-form per λ) |
| **Hyperparameters** | `n_estimators`, `learning_rate`, `max_depth`, `reg_lambda`, `subsample` | `kernel`, `lambda_grid`, `solver` |
| **Loss support** | `SquaredLoss`, `KLLoss`, `BernoulliLoss`, `BoundedSquaredLoss` | `SquaredLoss` only (today) |
| **Scaling** | Linear in $n$ via xgboost | $O(n^3)$ direct; Nyström + RFF + Falkon for larger $n$ |
| **Convenience class** | `rieszboost.RieszBooster` | `krrr.KernelRieszRegressor` |

## Compose explicitly

Both convenience classes are subclasses of `rieszreg.RieszEstimator` with the backend defaulted. To swap backends or build your own, compose explicitly:

```python
from rieszreg import RieszEstimator, ATE, SquaredLoss
from rieszboost.backends import XGBoostBackend, SklearnBackend
from krrr import KernelRidgeBackend, Gaussian

# Default: xgboost
est = RieszEstimator(estimand=ATE(), backend=XGBoostBackend())

# Sklearn boosting with KernelRidge as the base learner
from sklearn.kernel_ridge import KernelRidge
est = RieszEstimator(
    estimand=ATE(),
    backend=SklearnBackend(lambda: KernelRidge(alpha=1.0, kernel="rbf", gamma=2.0)),
)

# Kernel ridge directly
est = RieszEstimator(
    estimand=ATE(),
    backend=KernelRidgeBackend(kernel=Gaussian(length_scale="median")),
)
```

The convenience classes are thin subclasses that bake in the backend and surface backend-specific hyperparameters as constructor args, so that they fit cleanly into `GridSearchCV`. See the [boosting](boosting.qmd) and [kernel](kernel.qmd) pages for each.

## Adding a backend

Implement the `Backend` Protocol from [`rieszreg.backends.base`](https://github.com/alejandroschuler/RieszReg/blob/main/rieszreg/python/rieszreg/backends/base.py): one method, `fit_augmented(...)`, returning a `FitResult` whose `predictor` implements `predict_eta` and `predict_alpha`. Register the predictor's loader for round-trip via:

```python
from rieszreg.backends import register_predictor_loader
register_predictor_loader("my-kind", MyPredictor.load)
```

`XGBoostBackend`, `SklearnBackend`, and `KernelRidgeBackend` are reference implementations. See [Developing](../developing.qmd) for the full contract.
